description: Bot detection, CAPTCHA, CSRF protection, fraud prevention, DDoS mitigation

AEC.description: Ensures requests within a browsing session are made by the user and not by other sites

CMSCsrfCookie.description: Store's a security token that the system uses to validate all form data submitted via POST requests. Helps protect against Cross site request forgery.

G_AUTHUSER_H.description: Google multi-account sign-in cookie indicating which logged-in account to use for embedded services

Host-ERIC_PROD-.description: Enterprise Request Infrastructure Cookie (ERIC) carries the cross-site request forgery (CSRF) security token between the server and the client. The cookie name indicates the server mode (PROD or PRODDEBUG) and a random number. A different token is generated for each Lightning app.

INDEED_CSRF_TOKEN.description: This cookie is used by Cloudflare to identify trusted web traffic.

NID.description: Google NID cookie storing preferences and CAPTCHA verification for reCAPTCHA and Google services
NID.note: While categorized by Google as 'preferences', this cookie enables cross-site user identification

RT.description: Akamai mPulse real user monitoring round-trip time cookie for performance measurement

TS*.description: F5 Shape Security bot defense cookie for traffic classification and fraud prevention

ZCAMPAIGN_CSRF_TOKEN.description: This cookie is used to distinguish between humans and bots.

_GRECAPTCHA.description: Google reCAPTCHA challenge token — sets third-party cookie on google.com, leaking visitor IP and behavior data to Google
_GRECAPTCHA.note: Despite being 'security', this cookie enables Google cross-site tracking. Privacy alternatives: hCaptcha, Friendly Captcha, Cloudflare Turnstile

__AntiXsrfToken.description: This cookie is used to prevent Cross-site request forgery (often abbreviated as CSRF) attacks of the website. CSRF attacks exploit the trust that a site has in a user's browser.

__Host-GAPS.description: Google Account Protection Service cookie preventing cross-site request attacks on Google accounts

__Secure-ROLLOUT_TOKEN.description: Google A/B testing rollout token set on third-party domain — tracks feature flag assignments across sites
__Secure-ROLLOUT_TOKEN.note: Third-party cookie from youtube.com/google.com used for experiment bucketing — leaks browsing context to Google

__cf_bm.description: Identifies and distinguishes between humans and bots to mitigate automated abuse

__cf_turnstile_*.description: Cloudflare Turnstile privacy-preserving CAPTCHA challenge state cookie

__cflb.description: Cloudflare load balancer affinity cookie for routing requests to the same backend server

__cfruid.description: Cloudflare cookie for rate limiting identification to prevent denial-of-service attacks

__ddg1_.description: DuckDuckGo first-party cookie for preventing automated abuse while preserving privacy

__eoi.description: This cookie is used for security authenticate users, prevent fraud, and protect users as they interact with a service.

__imp_apg_r_*.description: Imperva/Incapsula bot mitigation cookie for WAF and DDoS protection

_abck.description: Akamai Bot Manager cookie for advanced bot detection using behavioral analysis

_cfuvid.description: Cloudflare unique visitor ID cookie for rate limiting per-visitor rather than per-IP

_dd_cookie_test_*.description: DataDome cookie test to verify browser cookie support for bot detection

_geetest_*.description: GeeTest CAPTCHA verification cookie for bot detection and challenge state

_grecaptcha.description: Google reCAPTCHA localStorage token — persists challenge state and behavioral fingerprint data on the client
_grecaptcha.note: Stored in localStorage, harder to clear than cookies. Contains device fingerprint signals

_px3.description: PerimeterX/HUMAN advanced bot detection cookie with behavioral analysis data

_pxde.description: PerimeterX/HUMAN debug-state cookie companion to _px3/_pxvid for bot detection

_pxhd.description: PerimeterX/HUMAN bot detection cookie storing browser fingerprint data

_pxvid.description: PerimeterX/HUMAN visitor identifier cookie for bot risk assessment

a0_users:sess.description: Used for CSRF protection in Classic Universal Login flows.

a0_users:sess.sig.description: Used for CSRF protection in Classic Universal Login flows.

aboutads_sessNNN.description: This cookie is used for security authenticate users, prevent fraud, and protect users as they interact with a service.

ak_bmsc.description: Akamai Bot Manager session cookie for distinguishing human from bot traffic

arkose_token.description: Arkose Labs fraud prevention token cookie for CAPTCHA challenge verification

bm_mi.description: Akamai Bot Manager machine identity cookie for advanced device fingerprinting

bm_sv.description: Akamai Bot Manager session verification cookie for real-time bot scoring

bm_sz.description: Akamai Bot Manager sizing cookie for recording client device capabilities for bot scoring

castle_session_*.description: Castle account security session cookie for device fingerprinting and anomaly detection

cf_clearance.description: Stores proof that a visitor has passed a Cloudflare challenge to avoid repeated challenges
cf_clearance.note: Functional security cookie, typically exempt from consent under GDPR Article 6(1)(f)

cf_ob_info.description: Cloudflare Always Online cookie storing origin error info for cached page delivery

csrf-canary.description: These cookies are used by the TrustPilot service to identify you and enable you to leave reviews of our products and services.

csrf_same_site.description: These cookies enable us to track visitor activity from our X ads on our website, and also to allow users to share content from our websites. They cookies do not provide us with any confidential information relating to your account.

csrf_same_site_set.description: These cookies enable us to track visitor activity from our X ads on our website, and also to allow users to share content from our websites. They cookies do not provide us with any confidential information relating to your account.

ct0.description: Stores a unique CSRF token to protect against cross-site request forgery on X/Twitter

datadome.description: DataDome persistent bot protection cookie storing device fingerprint and risk score

gist_oauth_csrf.description: This cookie is set by Gist to ensure the user that started the oauth flow is the same user that completes it.
gist_oauth_csrf.lifetime: Deleted when oauth state is validated

hs-membership-csrf.description: This cookie is used to ensure that content membership logins cannot be forged.

hubspotapi-csrf.description: This is used for CSRF prevention - preventing third party websites from accessing your data. Expires after a year.

idccsrf.description: Used for SSO authentication as CSRF protection.

incap_ses_*.description: Incapsula session cookie for maintaining security state during a visit

kasada_*.description: Kasada bot mitigation cookie for proof-of-work challenge verification

nfp_*.description: Netacea bot management fingerprint cookie for distinguishing legitimate traffic from automated bots

pxcts.description: PerimeterX/HUMAN counter cookie tracking client-side timestamps for bot risk scoring

reese84.description: F5 Shape Security bot detection cookie using telemetry data for automated threat classification

saml_csrf_token.description: This cookie is set by SAML auth path method to associate a token with the client.

sb.description: Stores browser details for Facebook security and login verification

shield_*.description: Shield Security WordPress plugin cookie for login protection, bot blocking, and firewall state

sucuri_cloudproxy_uuid_*.description: Sucuri CloudProxy WAF session cookie for maintaining security state during traffic filtering

visid_incap_*.description: Incapsula visitor identifier for bot detection and security analysis

wfwaf-authcookie-*.description: Wordfence WordPress WAF authentication cookie for bypassing firewall rules for logged-in admins

x-amz-captcha-1.description: Amazon CAPTCHA verification cookie for bot protection during security challenges

x-amz-captcha-2.description: Secondary Amazon CAPTCHA token for multi-step bot verification challenges

xf_csrf.description: This cookie is used to store a user's cross-site request forgery token, preventing other applications from making malicious requests on the user's behalf.

api.akismet.com.note: WordPress Akismet anti-spam plugin transmits comment data and IP for spam detection

api.cleantalk.org.note: CleanTalk anti-spam service sends visitor IP for spam database lookup

friendly-captcha.com.note: Privacy-friendly CAPTCHA alternative using proof-of-work; GDPR-compliant by design

mtcaptcha.com.note: GDPR-compliant CAPTCHA with EU-only data processing

prosopo.io.note: Web3-based CAPTCHA alternative using proof-of-work; privacy-first design

recaptcha.net.note: Google reCAPTCHA alternative domain used in regions where google.com is restricted

ssl.google-analytics.com.note: SSL endpoint for Google Analytics; may be used for consent verification

vercel.live.note: Vercel edge middleware for bot protection and rate limiting

www.google.com/recaptcha.note: reCAPTCHA leaks visitor IP and behavior data to Google. Privacy alternatives: hCaptcha, Friendly Captcha, Cloudflare Turnstile

www.google.com/recaptcha/enterprise.note: Enterprise reCAPTCHA with risk scoring; sets tracking cookies alongside security function
